title: nepeta argolica subsp. tumeniana
intro: Nepeta argolica subsp. tumeniana, also known as White Catmint or Mountain Catmint, is a subspecies of the Nepeta genus, a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant's medicinal properties also make it beneficial for respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis, and its antiseptic qualities can aid in wound healing. Nepeta argolica subsp. tumeniana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
